rulings.Case Title: HAROLD GERECKE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BILLY S. JOHNSON VS. ADVOCATE MINES, LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Cost Sought By Defendant Maremont Corporation
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 14, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to tax costs sought by defendant Maremont Corporation is granted in part and denied in part. All filing and motion fees claimed by defendant are taxed except $392 for the fees to file its answer and $522 for the fees to file its motion for summary judgment. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of the other claimed filing and motion fees were ¿reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.¿ (CCP 1033.5(c)(2)). All deposition costs claimed by defendant are taxed except $720.45 for volume 2 of James Shuman¿s deposition, $360.27 for volume 1 of James Shuman¿s deposition, $334.88 for volume 3 of James Shuman¿s deposition, $293.22 for volume 2 of the plaintiff¿s deposition, $164.97 for volume 1 of plaintiff¿s deposition and $213.77 for Thomas Nelson¿s deposition, The transcript of the summary judgment hearing is not a recoverable cost. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of the other claimed deposition costs were ¿reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.¿ All witness fees claimed by defendant are taxed. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of the witness fees are recoverable under the costs statutes and, even if they are recoverable, whether they were ¿reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.¿ All ¿other¿ costs claimed by defendant are taxed. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of these costs are recov
